approvalTitle: Revised Tasks: tail -f approach + tsc -b (4 tasks)
content:
# Tasks Document

- [ ] 1. Add `log` field to config interfaces and resolution
  - File: `src/config.ts`
  - Add optional `log?: string` to `AppConfig` interface (already done, verify)
  - Add optional `log?: string` to `ResolvedAppConfig` interface (already done, verify)
  - In `discoverConfig()`, resolve `log` relative to the app's resolved `cwd` (already done, verify)
  - Purpose: Enable apps to declare a log file path in `.pm3.json`
  - _Leverage: Existing path resolution pattern in src/config.ts_
  - _Requirements: 1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec pm3-log-command, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript Developer | Task: Verify that `log?: string` is present on both `AppConfig` and `ResolvedAppConfig` interfaces in src/config.ts, and that `discoverConfig()` resolves the `log` field relative to the app's resolved `cwd` following the same pattern as `script`. This was partially implemented — verify correctness and fix if needed. | Restrictions: Do not change any existing field behavior. Do not make `log` required. Keep the field undefined if not provided in config. | _Leverage: src/config.ts existing path resolution pattern for `script` | Success: AppConfig and ResolvedAppConfig have optional `log` field. When `.pm3.json` includes a `log` field, it is resolved to an absolute path relative to cwd. When omitted, the field is undefined. Project compiles. | Instructions: Mark this task as [-] in tasks.md before starting. After completion, log implementation with log-implementation tool, then mark as [x] in tasks.md._

- [ ] 2. Modify `startApp()` to redirect stdout/stderr to log file
  - File: `src/manager.ts`
  - When `app.log` is defined:
    - Create parent directory with `fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(app.log), { recursive: true })`
    - Open log file in append mode: `fs.openSync(app.log, 'a')`
    - Pass fd as stdio: `['ignore', logFd, logFd]`
    - Close fd after spawn: `fs.closeSync(logFd)`
  - When `app.log` is not defined: keep current `stdio: 'ignore'`
  - Purpose: Capture app stdout/stderr into log file on start
  - _Leverage: src/manager.ts existing startApp() function, Node.js fs module_
  - _Requirements: 2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec pm3-log-command, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Node.js Developer | Task: Modify `startApp()` in src/manager.ts so that when `app.log` is defined, stdout and stderr of the spawned process are redirected to the log file. Use `fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(app.log), { recursive: true })` to ensure the directory exists, `fs.openSync(app.log, 'a')` to open in append mode, pass `['ignore', logFd, logFd]` as stdio, and `fs.closeSync(logFd)` after spawn. When `app.log` is not defined, keep current `stdio: 'ignore'`. | Restrictions: Do not change detached/unref behavior. Do not modify stop/restart functions. Only touch the stdio portion of startApp. Import `fs` and `path` from node. | _Leverage: src/manager.ts startApp(), Node.js fs.openSync/closeSync | Success: When app has `log` field, `pm3 start` creates the log file directory if needed, and the spawned process writes stdout/stderr to the log file in append mode. Apps without `log` field behave as before. | Instructions: Mark this task as [-] in tasks.md before starting. After completion, log implementation with log-implementation tool, then mark as [x] in tasks.md._

- [ ] 3. Rewrite `logApps()` to tail log files using `tail -f`
  - File: `src/manager.ts`
  - Replace current `logApps()` implementation that spawns log scripts
  - Use `spawn('tail', ['-f', logPath])` since we run under Git Bash
  - Single app: spawn `tail -f` with `stdio: ['ignore', 'inherit', 'inherit']` (direct passthrough)
  - Multiple apps: spawn `tail -f` per app with piped stdio, use `readline` to prefix lines with `[app-name]`
  - SIGINT handler: kill all `tail` children, `process.exit(0)`
  - Purpose: Live-tail log files to terminal
  - _Leverage: src/manager.ts, spawn, tail -f (available via Git Bash)_
  - _Requirements: 3, 4_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec pm3-log-command, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Node.js Developer | Task: Rewrite `logApps()` in src/manager.ts to tail log files using `tail -f` instead of the old script-spawning approach. For single app: `spawn('tail', ['-f', app.log], { cwd: app.cwd, stdio: ['ignore', 'inherit', 'inherit'] })`. For multiple apps: spawn `tail -f` per app with piped stdio, use `node:readline` createInterface on stdout to prefix each line with `[appName]`. Track all ChildProcess instances. Register SIGINT handler to kill all children and process.exit(0). Filter apps to those with `log` field, error if none found. Remove the old fs.watchFile-based implementation. | Restrictions: Use `tail -f` via spawn (Git Bash environment). Do not use fs.watchFile or fs.watch. Keep ChildProcess import. | _Leverage: spawn from node:child_process, readline from node:readline | Success: `pm3 log appname` tails a single log file live. `pm3 log` tails all configured log files with [name] prefix. Ctrl+C cleanly kills all tail processes. Error shown for missing log field. | Instructions: Mark this task as [-] in tasks.md before starting. After completion, log implementation with log-implementation tool, then mark as [x] in tasks.md._

- [ ] 4. Build and end-to-end validation
  - Files: No new files
  - Run `npx tsc -b tsconfig.json` to compile
  - Create test `.pm3.json` with apps that have `log` fields pointing to log files
  - Test: `pm3 start` writes output to log files, `pm3 log` tails them live
  - Verify: Ctrl+C cleanup, error messages for missing log field, multi-app prefixed output
  - Fix any issues found
  - Purpose: Ensure the full log feature works end-to-end
  - _Leverage: All source files_
  - _Requirements: All_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec pm3-log-command, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: QA Engineer | Task: Build the project with `npx tsc -b tsconfig.json`. Create a test setup with a .pm3.json containing apps with `log` fields. Test: (1) `pm3 start` redirects app output to log files, (2) `pm3 log app1` shows live output from one app's log file, (3) `pm3 log` shows prefixed output from all apps' log files, (4) Ctrl+C cleanly exits, (5) error for app without log field, (6) log file created with parent dirs. Fix any bugs found during testing. | Restrictions: Only fix bugs — do not change architecture. | Success: All scenarios pass, log files populated on start, tailing works live, clean Ctrl+C. | Instructions: Mark this task as [-] in tasks.md before starting. After completion, log implementation with log-implementation tool, then mark as [x] in tasks.md._
